channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Entire Chicago school board to resign over teachers union dispute with Dem mayor: 'Deeply alarming'
item.description: The entire Chicago Board of Education is resigning after reportedly declining to fire the Chicago Public Schools CEO during contract negotiations with the city's teachers union.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For small cities across Alabama with Haitian populations, Springfield is a cautionary tale
item.description: Many small Alabama cities have been beset by the same polarizing debate that thrust Springfield, Ohio, into the spotlight after former president Donald Trump promoted debunked misinformation about Haitian migrants.
item.dc:creator: Safiyah Riddle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, Fox 5

item.title: What happens to items surrendered to TSA?
item.description: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) said it does not confiscate items. According to the agency, if someone shows up with a prohibited item, they are given a choice on what to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court opens its new term with election disputes in the air but not yet on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Transgender rights, the regulation of “ghost guns” and the death penalty highlight the Supreme Court's election-season term that begins Monday, with the prospect of the court's intervention in voting disputes lurking in the background.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dallas Uber driver seen on video punching passenger after dispute over legroom
item.description: A 68-year-old Uber passenger was punched in the back of the head in Dallas, Texas, after he complained about the lack of legroom inside a car, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Well-known Asheville music tradition returns in a sign of hopefulness after Helene
item.description: A well-known Asheville musical tradition has returned in a sign of hopefulness a week after Helene battered the mountain city.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dolly Parton, Walmart announce millions in donations to Helene victims
item.description: East Tennessee's own Dolly Parton is expected to offer her assistance to help with the flood recovery efforts in Newport.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, Fox 5

item.title: Some perplexed at jury's mixed verdict in trial for 3 former officers in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Moments after a federal judge read the partial convictions in the federal trial of three former Memphis officers in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, an activist outside the courtroom could not contain his shock. “What the (expletive)?” Hunter Demster exclaimed to a reporter.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Several bands drop out of Orlando heavy metal festival after organizers book Kyle Rittenhouse as guest
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. — An upcoming heavy metal festival set to take place in Florida this month is undergoing some major lineup changes after several bands that were set to perform at the event dropped out because Kyle Rittenhouse had been invited to participate.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arrests made in Memphis kidnapping and violent robbery
item.description: A man told police he was kidnapped at gunpoint from the Fair View Inn on Sycamore View Road by two men and two women.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to menus for a limited time
item.description: The sandwich is essentially built the same way as the Big Mac, albeit with two "tempura-battered chicken patties"
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal after report that only Trump's might qualify
item.description: Oklahoma Education Superintendent Ryan Walters’s (R) office is defending the process for how the state will be selecting newly mandated Bibles in classrooms after a report was released that the criteria is so narrow that essentially no Bibles qualify — besides ones endorsed by former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: US launches airstrikes on Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels
item.description: Military aircraft and warships bombed Houthi strongholds at roughly five locations, according to the officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Mayorkas' claim that FEMA is 'tremendously prepared' comes back to haunt him amid Helene aftermath
item.description: Parts of North Carolina and South Carolina have been completely submerged by flooding and communities nearly wiped out by Hurricane Helene, with residents waiting for FEMA help days later.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5,000 gallons of hot asphalt spilled on Texas interstate
item.description: "We ask for you to keep the victim and their family in your thoughts and prayers," the Lindale Fire Department said.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Escatel, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Need a passport? You can now get one faster
item.description: U.S. passport processing time has been reduced by two weeks at most, the State Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Bigfoot footage goes viral on social media
item.description: In what closely resembles a scene from a 1980s movie or a beef jerky commercial, new footage purportedly of Bigfoot has racked up millions of views on social media. The video was recorded in the Parallel Forest in Lawton, Oklahoma, on Sept. 27.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: First EV 'gas station' opens in Southern California
item.description: Gas stations are great… but what if you have an electric car? A new type of service station is opening up in Santa Ana, complete with lots of EV chargers, a car wash, a WiFi lounge and even a mini-Gelsons for food and snacks.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: US adds robust 254,000 jobs; unemployment dips to 4.1%
item.description: It's the latest evidence that the U.S. labor market is still solid enough to support steady hiring and a growing economy.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, WGN 9

item.title: Motorized wheelchair stuffed with cocaine seized at Bush airport, CBP said
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) -- Unusually large cushions on a motorized wheelchair piqued the interest of officers at George Bush Intercontinental Airport last month in Houston, U.S. Customs and Border Protection said. Their suspicions were right.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: NYC accelerates school leadership change as investigations swirl around mayor’s indictment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City is speeding up its switch to a new schools chief as indicted Mayor Eric Adams faces mounting pressure to bring stability to a city government that has been roiled by searches, subpoenas and resignations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: Shipping container slams into electrical pole during Hurricane Helene; death toll reaches 200
item.description: A shipping container slammed into an electrical pole while floating debris was split in half during extreme, rising water in Asheville, North Carolina last week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas casino implosion show will include 150 'Pyrodrones' 400 feet above the Tropicana
item.description: Las Vegas tourists should not run in fear as 150 “Pyrdrones” shoot sparks 400 feet above the Tropicana demolition site next week—because it’s all part of the show.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, Fox 5

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: The former first lady said Wednesday that she thinks Americans need to listen to one another, even if they hold different beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Helene's victims include first responders who died helping others
item.description: Falling trees and raging floods from Hurricane Helene killed more than 150 people when it struck Florida late last week and then plowed through the Southeast as one of the deadliest storms in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
